name: Ofe Owerri
intro: The soup may thicken a lot because of the cocoyam. You can add extra water until you reach the desired consistency.

ingredients.item: small red cocoyams (approx. 500g) (peeled and cut into large chunks)

method: Place the cocoyam in a large pot, cover with water and boil over high heat for 30 minutes until tender. Leave to cool for 20 minutes. Place the cocoyam in a food blender and blend with 1 cup of warm water (you can use the water that the cocoyam was cooked in) until smooth.

Place the meat alternatives in a large pot with 2 cups of water (If using tofu, add towards the end of the cooking time). Bring to a boil then add the blended cocoyam a little at a time, mixing as you go along. Rinse out the blender with half a cup of water and pour into the pot. Turn the heat down to medium.

Add the palm oil, ogiri, vegetable stock cube, chilli powder/ cameroon pepper, ugu and ukazi leaves. Stir well to combine and simmer on medium heat for 5 minutes. Taste for seasoning and add salt if necessary.
Serve with a swallow of your choice such as eba or fufu.
